$comment: Kazakhstan conflict ledger — validates against schemas/geo/conflict.schema.json. Conflicts are never hidden and never resolved toward the higher-converting answer. Critical unresolved conflicts block CTA (Gate 03) and first place (Gate 04).

conflicts.claim: MEXC is available/permitted for Kazakhstan residents
conflicts.resolution: UNCERTAIN — both facts are true on their own level: MEXC's own terms (Tier A, 2025-05-29) do not restrict Kazakhstan, but the local regulator (Tier A, 2026-04-29) names MEXC as unlicensed and Kazakhstan actively blocks unlicensed platforms. Practical accessibility from KZ is untested (Phase 3 blocked on PROXY_KZ). Regulatory safety takes precedence for any CTA/ranking decision; owner review required.

conflicts.claim: OKX is available/permitted for Kazakhstan residents
conflicts.resolution: UNCERTAIN — OKX's own current disclosure (Tier A, updated 2026-07-08) does not restrict Kazakhstan, but the local regulator (Tier A, 2026-04-29) names OKX as unlicensed and Kazakhstan actively blocks unlicensed platforms. Practical accessibility from KZ is untested (Phase 3 blocked on PROXY_KZ). Regulatory safety takes precedence for any CTA/ranking decision; owner review required.

conflicts.claim: Bitget serves Kazakhstan users despite its own prohibition
conflicts.resolution: Resolved by official-source precedence: Sprint 8B noted third-party P2P trackers showing live Bitget KZT order flow (unverified Tier C/D market observation, not admitted as ledger evidence), but Bitget's OWN current terms (2026-06-16) explicitly prohibit Kazakhstan AND the regulator names Bitget as unlicensed. Two independent Tier-A sources agree: NOT available. Observed grey-market activity does not override official terms.
